analysis.ambiguity1:
Requirement: "The app should force users to select a safe zone before they can chat."

Why vague: The term "force" is not defined — it is unclear whether the system should block the chat feature entirely or just display a warning. Additionally, there is no definition of what qualifies as a "Safe Exchange Zone" or who is responsible for maintaining that list.
analysis.ambiguity2:
Requirement: "I want to be able to auction items, not just set a fixed price."

Why vague: The auction mechanism is not defined — there is no specification for minimum bid increment, auction duration, how a winner is determined, or what happens if no bids are placed before the deadline.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: The University Legal Team states the app cannot process any payments, as the university is not a bank. However, the Student Union President wants to collect a 2% transaction fee to fund club activities.

Solution: The app will not process payments directly. Instead, it will display a suggested voluntary contribution of 2% to the Student Union fund, with payment handled externally via Venmo or cash. This keeps the university legally clear while still supporting club funding goals.
analysis.nfr1: Security: Only users with a valid .edu email address may register. All accounts must be verified before accessing the marketplace to prevent unauthorized external users.
analysis.nfr2: Safety: The system must enforce selection of a Safe Exchange Zone before enabling chat between buyers and sellers to ensure all physical meetups occur in designated safe locations.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: The app must support barcode scanning for listing items, allowing students to create listings quickly without manually entering product details such as ISBN or title.
